rulings.Case Title: MIKE PASINI VS. SALVADOR GARCIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Prevailing Party And Award Of Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 9, 2024, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MIKE PASINI's Motion For Determination Of Prevailing Party And Award Of Attorney Fees. Ordered off calendar for defendant's noncompliance with Local Rule 2.7(B) (courtesy copies). Motion may be re-set for a Mon.-Thur. after October 24. Defendant's papers shall bear new hearing date. Plaintiff complied with Rule 2.7(B) and need not re-file his papers. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: RONIN PRODUCT DEVELOPMENT LABS, LLC, VS. VEBU LABS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 9, 2024, Line 2, MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD AS TO DEFENDANT VEBU LABS, INC. Prospera Law. LLP and Shame Tseng's unopposed motion to be relieved as counsel is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed judicial council order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: HUMANMADE, A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT CORPORATION VS. 150 HOOPER, INC., ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 9, 2024, Line 10, DEFENDANT 150 HOOPER, INC.'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. This demurrer to a third amended complaint was transferred from the Real Property Department (which ruled on demurrers to the two prior versions of the complaint). Much of the parties' disputation relates to what the prior versions did or did not say. Thus, the parties shall meet and confer to create redlined versions showing the complaint's evolution through its first, second and third amendments. Also, plaintiff relies on the Real Property Department's rulings in what it says in a related unlawful detainer action. Each party shall state its position on the import of that case in a brief of no more than five pages. When the above tasks are complete, the parties may calendar a case management conference in Department 302. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY ROBERTS VS. REDMINT, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 9, 2024, Line 7, 1- DEFENDANT REDMINT ONE, LLC's Motion To Compel Arbitration. Defendant Redmint One, LLC's motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied. Plaintiff pleads a claim for sexual harassment and therefore the action is not arbitrable under the federal Ending Forced Arbitration of Sexual Assault and Sexual Harassment Act ("EFAA"). The EFAA voids a pre-dispute arbitration agreement "with respect to a case which . . .relates to . . . the sexual harassment dispute." (9 U.S.C. 402(a) (emphasis added).) Here, plaintiff pleads a cognizable claim for sexual harassment. The court liberally construes plaintiff's complaint per CCP 452. In 2019, the California Legislature clarified harassment law and noted "[a] single incident of harassing conduct is sufficient to create a triable issue regarding the existence of a hostile work environment if the harassing conduct has unreasonably interfered with the plaintiff's work performance or created an intimidating, hostile, or offensive work environment." (Government Code section 12923(b); see also Beltran v. Hard Rock Hotel Licensing, Inc. (2023) 97 Cal.App.5th 865, 878-880 [noting less demanding standard and inapplicability of former cases]; CACI 2524 [defining severe or pervasive]; Bailey v. San Francisco District Attorney's Office (2024) 16 Cal.5th 611 [use of single racial epithet could constitute severe conduct to support harassment claim].) Plaintiff alleges several specific instances of objectionable conduct and notes that the conduct spanned "at least an eight-month period." (Complaint, pars. 21-24, 38.) Plaintiff alleges a viable sexual harassment claim and defendant largely relies on outdated cases to argue otherwise. As alluded to above, the case is exempt from arbitration. Thus, there is no basis to sever plaintiff's non-harassment claims and compel them to arbitration. (See Turner v. Tesla, Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2023) 686 F.Supp.3d 917, 925 ["[T]he text of Sec. 402(a) makes clear that its invalidation of an arbitration agreement extends to the entirety of the case"].) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
